description_long: This house is a three-story residential building with a rectangular footprint and a flat, slightly tiered roof. The exterior walls are finished in a muted beige stucco, accented by a section of darker wood paneling on one corner, adding visual interest. Rows of arched windows are symmetrically arranged across the upper floors, while the ground floor features larger rectangular windows and a main entry door. The roofline is flat with subtle stepped detailing, and the building's facade is punctuated by simple horizontal trim bands. The structure appears robust, likely constructed from masonry or concrete, with wood and glass elements for windows and trim. The overall design is utilitarian with some classical influences, suitable for an urban neighborhood setting.
description: A three-story beige house with a flat, tiered roof, symmetrical arched windows, and wood panel accents. The facade is simple yet sturdy, with classical trim and a prominent entryway.
description_view_0: This view shows the narrow side of the house, highlighting the stepped roof and a section of wood paneling with several windows.
description_view_1: This side view displays the long facade with two rows of arched windows and a wood-paneled corner, emphasizing the building's length and symmetry.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow side is visible here, featuring fewer windows and a more uniform beige stucco finish.
description_view_3: The main facade is shown, with the entry door, large ground-floor windows, and symmetrical rows of arched windows above, as well as the wood-paneled corner.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with a single door, no porch, and simple trim; ground-level access with minimal decorative elements.
